metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students examine child development stages, early childhood education theory and practice, and professional caregiving standards. This course prepares students for childcare, early childhood education, teaching, and related professions through understanding developmental psychology and caregiving practices.
metadata.general_description: Child development stages, early childhood education, caregiving practices, and child-related professions.

big_ideas.statement: Children develop through predictable yet individual pathways across physical, cognitive, social, and emotional domains
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding developmental stages and milestones enables caregivers to support appropriate growth and identify potential concerns.

big_ideas.statement: Early childhood experiences significantly influence lifelong learning, health, and social-emotional development
big_ideas.elaboration: Quality early childhood programs provide foundational experiences that support later academic success and well-being.

big_ideas.statement: Effective caregiving combines knowledge of child development with compassion, patience, and responsive interaction
big_ideas.elaboration: Professional caregivers create nurturing environments that meet children's physical needs while supporting cognitive and emotional growth.

big_ideas.statement: Families and communities shape children's development within cultural and socioeconomic contexts
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding diverse family structures, cultures, and circumstances helps caregivers provide inclusive, respectful, and culturally responsive care.

competencies.raw: Design learning environments and activities that support child development
i_can_statements: I can design classrooms and play spaces that are safe and developmentally appropriate
I can plan activities that support physical, cognitive, and social development
I can design inclusive environments for children with diverse needs
I can create structured routines that support learning and well-being

competencies.raw: Design programs and curricula for early childhood education
i_can_statements: I can develop age-appropriate curriculum frameworks
I can design thematic units and learning projects
I can plan assessment and documentation strategies
I can integrate play-based and academic learning

competencies.raw: Design family engagement and partnership strategies
i_can_statements: I can develop communication systems with families
I can plan family involvement and volunteer opportunities
I can design parent education and support programs
I can create culturally responsive family partnerships

competencies.raw: Design guidance and behavior support strategies
i_can_statements: I can develop positive discipline and guidance approaches
I can design conflict resolution and problem-solving strategies
I can plan interventions for challenging behaviors
I can create supportive responses to social-emotional needs

competencies.raw: Apply knowledge of child development to understand and support children
i_can_statements: I can identify developmental milestones and stages
I can recognize typical and atypical development patterns
I can match activities and expectations to developmental levels
I can support children's learning and growth

competencies.raw: Apply caregiving skills to meet children's physical, cognitive, and emotional needs
i_can_statements: I can provide safe, hygienic care routines
I can respond sensitively to children's emotional needs
I can facilitate learning through play and exploration
I can model and teach appropriate behaviors

competencies.raw: Apply observation and assessment techniques to understand children's progress
i_can_statements: I can observe and document children's behavior and learning
I can identify individual strengths and needs
I can use assessment results to plan appropriate activities
I can communicate observations to families

competencies.raw: Apply communication and relationship-building skills with children and families
i_can_statements: I can use positive, age-appropriate communication
I can build trusting relationships with children
I can communicate respectfully with families
I can resolve concerns and conflicts professionally

competencies.raw: Use technology to enhance early childhood education
i_can_statements: I can use educational apps and software appropriately
I can incorporate multimedia into learning activities
I can use technology to support diverse learners
I can balance screen time with other activities
I can use portfolio and documentation systems
I can organize and analyze observation data
I can use photography and video ethically
I can create digital learning records

competencies.raw: Use communication technology for family engagement
i_can_statements: I can use secure messaging for family communication
I can create digital newsletters and updates
I can manage family information securely
I can provide virtual learning opportunities

competencies.raw: Use technology to support inclusive and accessible learning
i_can_statements: I can use assistive technology for children with disabilities
I can apply universal design principles
I can adapt technology for diverse learners
I can support multilingual development through technology

areas.name: Child Development Across Domains
topics: Physical development and motor skill milestones

areas.name: Early Childhood Education Theory and Practice
areas.raw: Early Childhood Education Theory and Practice
topics: Early childhood education approaches and philosophies

areas.name: Caregiving, Health, and Safety
topics: Physical care routines and hygiene practices
Safety, accident prevention, and first aid
Health screening and identifying developmental delays
